message.content:
# Knowledge Base Update Notes: `marked` (npm)

## Package Overview
- **Purpose:** Markdown-to-HTML parser for JavaScript.
- **Popularity:** ~38M weekly downloads (as of mid-April 2026); widely adopted.

## Critical Security Design Note
- **Marked does NOT sanitize output HTML.** The README explicitly warns users to pair `marked` with a dedicated sanitizer (DOMPurify recommended, or sanitize-html/insane). Failure to do so exposes applications to XSS via crafted Markdown input.
- The `sanitize` and `sanitizer` options were **deprecated in v0.7.0** and should not be relied upon.

## Known Vulnerabilities (OSV/CVE)

### XSS / Content Injection
| Advisory | CVE | Fixed In | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| GHSA-9cw2-jqp5-7x39 | CVE-2014-3743 | 0.3.1 | Multiple content injection vulnerabilities |
| GHSA-cfjh-p3g4-3q2f | CVE-2015-1370 | 0.3.3 | VBScript content injection |
| GHSA-vfvf-mqq8-rwqc | CVE-2016-10531 | 0.3.6 | Sanitization bypass via HTML entities |
| GHSA-7px7-7xjx-hxm8 | CVE-2017-1000427 | 0.3.7 | XSS via data URIs |

### Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
| Advisory | CVE | Fixed In | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| GHSA-hjcp-j389-59ff | CVE-2015-8854 | 0.3.4 | ReDoS |
| GHSA-x5pg-88ch-gxw2 | CVE-2017-16114 | 0.3.9 | ReDoS |
| GHSA-p9wx-2529-fp83 | CVE-2018-25110 | 0.3.17 | ReDoS |
| GHSA-xf5p-87ch-gxw2 | — | 0.6.2 | ReDoS (email addresses, quadratic time) |
| GHSA-ch52-vgq2-943f | — | 0.7.0 | ReDoS (links with backticks) |
| GHSA-4r62-v4vq-hr96 | CVE-2021-21306 | 2.0.0 | ReDoS |
| GHSA-5v2h-r2cx-5xgj | CVE-2022-21681 | 4.0.10 | Inefficient regex complexity |
| GHSA-rrrm-qjm4-v8hf | CVE-2022-21680 | 4.0.10 | Inefficient regex complexity |

## Watershed Versions
- **v0.3.x series (0.3.1–0.3.17):** Successive patches for XSS and ReDoS; all pre-0.3.17 versions carry known vulnerabilities.
- **v0.6.2:** Fixed email-address ReDoS (quadratic complexity).
- **v0.7.0:** Fixed link-backtick ReDoS; **deprecated `sanitize`/`sanitizer` options** (breaking change); moved fences to CommonMark, tables to GFM.
- **v2.0.0:** Fixed CVE-2021-21306 ReDoS; major version boundary.
- **v4.0.10:** Fixed the last two known ReDoS advisories (CVE-2022-21680, CVE-2022-21681). **This is the minimum version with no publicly tracked vulnerabilities.**

## Security Posture Notes
- **Disclosure policy:** SECURITY.md commits to initial assessment within 48 hours and patches within 2 weeks; email-based private disclosure to committers and npm owners.
- The historical vulnerability pattern is dominated by **ReDoS** (8 of 12 advisories), reflecting the regex-heavy nature of Markdown parsing.
- The project's explicit stance ("The only completely secure system is the one that doesn't exist") and prominent XSS warning suggest mature awareness but also an intentional design boundary: **output sanitization is the consumer's responsibility.**

## Open Questions
1. **Post-4.0.10 security posture:** No new OSV advisories are listed beyond 4.0.10, but it is unknown whether regex complexity has been comprehensively audited in subsequent major versions (e.g., v5+, v6+, if released).
2. **Sanitize option removal:** The `sanitize` option was deprecated in 0.7.0. It is unclear from available evidence whether it has been fully removed in later majors, or if legacy users may still inadvertently rely on it
